name: inv_allows_zero_layers
code:
proof fn phi_1_inv_allows_zero_layers(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: Arch::inv should require at least one layer; a zero-layer architecture is degenerate and unusable for address translation

name: entry_base_overlap
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_overlap(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: Distinct entry indices at the same layer must map to distinct base addresses; overlap would corrupt address translation

name: interp_of_entry_key_overlap
code:
proof fn phi_3_interp_of_entry_key_overlap(d: Directory, i: nat, j: nat, va: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        j < d.entries.len(),
        i != j,
        d.interp_of_entry(i).contains_key(va),
    ensures
        d.interp_of_entry(j).contains_key(va),
{
}
reason: Two different directory entries should never map the same virtual address; overlapping keys would cause ambiguous translations

name: well_formed_allows_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_4_well_formed_allows_zero_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.num_entries() == 0,
{
}
reason: A well-formed directory with zero entries is degenerate; well_formed should guarantee at least one slot via arch.inv

name: contains_zero_entry_size
code:
proof fn phi_5_contains_zero_entry_size(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() > 0,
    ensures
        arch.contains_entry_size_at_index_atleast(0, 0),
{
}
reason: Under a valid arch invariant all entry sizes are positive, so finding an entry_size of 0 would indicate an inconsistent spec
